title: A water heating and treating device
abstract: A water heating and treating device is disclosed for obtaining water with known conductivity from water from a water supply, which water supply is substantially stable with respect to the conductivity, temperature, flow rate and solubility/consistence of the supplied water, wherein the water heating and treating device is constituted by a rectangular body with tracks for electrodes, with a lid with tracks for electrodes comprising 2 sides of electrodes which is arranged so that, they are mounted in the body and also held by the lid leaving enough volume for water to pass by the electrodes connected to AC power from the circuit board, via the in- and outlet of the device.
claims: Claims Claims 1 ) A water heating and treating device for water with known conductivity from a water supply, which water supply is substantially stable with respect to the conductivity, temperature, flow rate and solubility/, wherein the water heating and treating device is constituted by a rectangular body with tracks for electrodes, with a lid with tracks for electrodes comprising 2 sides of electrodes which is arranged so that, they are mounted in the body and also held by the lid leaving enough volume for water to pass by the electrodes connected to AC power from the circuit board, via the in- and outlet of the device. 2) The water heating and treating device according to claim 1 , wherein the electrodes area of watt effect is created by inserting an insulator between the electrodes on 2 sides. 3) The water heating and treating device according to claim 1 or 2, wherein, the water in- and outlets are placed asymmetrical opposite each other to make use of different size and shape isolator between the electrodes making it possible to use fixed sized electrodes and create a certain watt effect. 4) The water heating and treating device according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the design makes sure that the electrodes do not touch as the insulator and the tracks in the lid and in the body secures that there are no possibility for the zero and phase electrodes to touch each other. 5) The water heating and treating device according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the volume of water between the electrodes are kept to maximum 1 mm between the phase and zero of a power supply. 6) The water heating and treating device according to any of the preceding claims, wherein, the electrodes are made of 20/20 mesh developed graphite material, with surfaces polished in a way that makes the material withstand sediment that would otherwise stick to the electrodes. 7) The water heating and treating device according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the electrodes are positioned with isolator material between them on minimum 2 sides and above them so the phase electrodes become a grid like structure. 8) The water heating or treating device according to any of the 5 preceding claims, wherein non conducting push in connectors are used for water in and outlet. 9) The water heating or treating device according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the RO - reverse osmosis water is heated.
